questions.question_text: Under T.C.A. § 62-13-312, which action is grounds for discipline by TREC?
questions.correct_answer: Commingling trust funds with personal funds
distractors: Charging a commission rate above 6% to sellers
Refusing to list a property below market value
Advertising a listed property at market value
questions.explanation: Commingling trust funds with personal or business funds is a serious violation under the License Act. Agents must keep client funds separate in a designated trust account.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker pays a $500 referral fee to an unlicensed friend who referred a buyer. What is the consequence?
distractors: Acceptable if the amount is under $1,000
Permitted if disclosed to the buyer
Legal if the friend signs a waiver
questions.explanation: Paying referral fees to unlicensed persons is expressly listed as grounds for discipline under T.C.A. § 62-13-312. The amount and disclosure do not change this prohibition.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT listed as grounds for TREC discipline?
questions.correct_answer: Charging a rate above the market average
distractors: Making false promises to a client
Failing to maintain E&O insurance
Acting for both parties without consent
questions.explanation: Commission rates are negotiable and not regulated by TREC. False promises, failing to maintain E&O, and undisclosed dual agency are all grounds for discipline under the License Act.

questions.question_text: A licensee is convicted of mortgage fraud. Is this grounds for TREC discipline?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, criminal conviction related to real estate
distractors: No, only direct TREC rule violations apply
Only if the conviction was a felony charge
Only if TREC itself was the original complainant
questions.explanation: Criminal conviction related to real estate activities is specifically listed as grounds for discipline. TREC can take action based on the conviction itself, regardless of who filed the complaint.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker fails to provide the buyer with copies of the signed purchase agreement. What violation has occurred?
questions.correct_answer: Failing to provide document copies to parties
distractors: Unauthorized practice of real estate law
Commingling of client trust account funds
Breach of the fiduciary duty of loyalty
questions.explanation: Failing to provide copies of signed documents to all parties is a specific ground for discipline under T.C.A. § 62-13-312. Licensees must provide copies at the time of signing.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a disciplinary action TREC can impose?
distractors: Criminal imprisonment
Monetary damages to the buyer
Foreclosure on the licensee's home
questions.explanation: TREC can impose license revocation, suspension, civil penalties, reprimand, and probation. Criminal penalties and monetary damages to individuals are handled by courts, not TREC.

questions.question_text: A licensee commits a minor first-time violation of TREC advertising rules. What is the LEAST severe action TREC might take?
distractors: License revocation
Two-year suspension
Permanent license denial
questions.explanation: TREC has a range of disciplinary options from least to most severe: reprimand, probation, civil penalties, suspension, and revocation. A reprimand is appropriate for minor first-time violations.

questions.question_text: In addition to license actions, what monetary penalty can TREC impose?
distractors: Criminal fines through the court
Treble damages to the consumer
Punitive damages to the complainant
questions.explanation: TREC can impose civil penalties as part of its disciplinary authority. Criminal fines and damages to consumers must be pursued through the court system, not through TREC.

questions.question_text: TREC places a licensee on probation with conditions. If the licensee violates probation terms, what can TREC do?
questions.correct_answer: Escalate discipline up to license revocation
distractors: Only extend the existing probation period
Issue a written warning but take no action
Refer the entire matter to civil court only
questions.explanation: TREC can escalate discipline if probation terms are violated. This may include suspension or revocation of the license, as the licensee has demonstrated continued non-compliance.

questions.question_text: TREC learns an unlicensed person is showing homes for compensation. What immediate action can TREC take?
questions.correct_answer: Issue a cease and desist order
distractors: Suspend the person's license
Place the person on probation
Fine the person's principal broker
questions.explanation: TREC can issue cease and desist orders against unlicensed individuals. Suspension and probation only apply to licensees. The person has no license to suspend.

questions.question_text: In Tennessee, practicing real estate without a license is classified as what type of offense?
distractors: A civil infraction only
An administrative violation only
A regulatory misdemeanor only
questions.explanation: Unlicensed practice of real estate is a criminal offense in Tennessee, carrying potential criminal penalties beyond just administrative action by TREC.

questions.question_text: A homeowner sells their own property without a real estate license. Is this considered unlicensed practice?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners may sell their own property
distractors: Yes, a license is always required to sell
Only if the property exceeds $250,000
Only if the owner advertises publicly
questions.explanation: Property owners are exempt from licensing requirements when selling their own property. The license requirement applies to those who conduct real estate activities for others for compensation.

questions.question_text: An apartment complex employee collects rent and handles maintenance requests as part of their salary. Do they need a license?
questions.correct_answer: No, employees of owners are typically exempt
distractors: Yes, all property managers need a license
Only if managing more than 10 units
Only if their salary exceeds $50,000
questions.explanation: Employees of property owners who perform management tasks as part of their regular duties are typically exempt from the licensing requirement. The exemption applies to salaried employees, not independent contractors.

questions.question_text: A person negotiates sales and collects fees for arranging real estate transactions for others. What license is required?
distractors: A business license is sufficient
No license if they call themselves a consultant
A referral agent registration only
questions.explanation: Negotiating sales and collecting fees for arranging real estate transactions for others constitutes licensed activity requiring a Tennessee real estate license. The title used does not change the requirement.

questions.question_text: Which activity would require a Tennessee real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Listing properties for sale for compensation
distractors: Selling your own personal residence
Providing legal advice about a deed
Appraising property for a bank
questions.explanation: Listing properties for sale on behalf of others for compensation is a core real estate activity requiring licensure. Selling one's own property, legal advice (attorney), and appraisal (appraiser) have different licensing requirements.
